notes: Third Aberdeen SOA study; central thesis: SOA distributes and decouples processing from data center to network edge and anywhere in-between.
Network traffic moving from data packets to XML-based content routing and Web Services request messaging; blurs line between network and software architecture.
SOA middleware delivered inside XML-aware appliances; network infrastructure taking on new roles. Kastner identifies this as a key architectural shift.
Central to SOA messaging; network traffic shifting to Web Services request messaging; Aberdeen benchmarking state of practice for Web Services-based network applications.

Core topic of this study: Aberdeen will benchmark state of practice for application processing on the network; types of applications suited for network processing; benefits early adopters are realizing.
Study will cover SOA security in Web Services and XML processing — identified as key research area.
